meta.title: 2026 NFL Teams Digital Twin Dataset
meta.description: Complete digital twin data for all 32 NFL teams for the Per|Form draft simulation engine
meta.notes: Data sourced from NFL.com, ESPN, CBS Sports, and team websites as of April 2026. Fields marked unverified require additional research.

draft_order_round_1.team: Kansas City Chiefs (from Los Angeles Rams)

ownership.name: Mark Davis (minority owner: Tom Brady)
ownership.style: Hands-on but delegating to new front office; willing to spend on marquee moves
ownership.tendencies: Historically unstable coaching carousel; new regime signals desire for long-term stability; Brady's minority stake adds strategic football influence

gm.name: John Spytek
gm.philosophy: BPA-leaning with emphasis on building through the draft; willing to make bold trades
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — modern analytics integrated with traditional scouting

head_coach.name: Klint Kubiak
head_coach.scheme_offense: Outside zone-heavy scheme (Kubiak/Shanahan tree), play-action passing, bootlegs
head_coach.scheme_defense: 3-4 base defense (transitioning from 4-3)

decision_makers.primary: John Spytek (GM) with Klint Kubiak (HC) input

roster_2026.qb_situation: Drafting Fernando Mendoza at #1 overall; signed veteran Kirk Cousins as bridge/mentor; Kubiak prefers rookie to learn behind veteran
key_departures: Maxx Crosby (trade fell through with Ravens, still disgruntled)
roster_2026.cap_space: Moderate — spent significantly in free agency but still have room

compensatory: unverified — multiple late-round comp picks expected

needs.reason: Shifting to 3-4 defense requires reloading the DT room; unquestionably the weakest position on the roster
Outside of Brock Bowers (TE) and Ashton Jeanty (RB), receiving corps is thin; Tre Tucker and Jalen Nailor are current top options
Upgrading right tackle would make O-line near-complete, critical for protecting Mendoza and opening lanes for Jeanty

ownership.name: Woody Johnson
ownership.style: Hands-on, impatient with losing; historically meddlesome in football decisions
ownership.tendencies: Quick to fire coaches; has cycled through many GMs and HCs; splashy free-agent signings; win-now mentality despite rebuild-level roster

gm.name: Darren Mougey
gm.philosophy: Building through the draft; needs to hit on early picks to establish credibility
gm.analytics_approach: unverified

head_coach.name: Aaron Glenn
head_coach.tenure: 2nd year (retained for 2026 after 3-14 in first season)
head_coach.scheme_offense: OC Frank Reich calling plays; West Coast concepts with vertical shots
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Brian Duker; aggressive man-coverage scheme; desperate for playmakers and takeaways (only 4 takeaways in 2025, fewest since 1940)

decision_makers.primary: Darren Mougey (GM) with Aaron Glenn (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Acquired Geno Smith via trade from Raiders; looking for long-term answer
key_departures: Jermaine Johnson II (EDGE, traded to Titans)
Sauce Gardner (traded to Colts for 2026+2027 first-round picks + Adonai Mitchell)
key_signings: Minkah Fitzpatrick (S, acquired via trade from Dolphins, signed 3yr/$40M extension)

picks: Round 1 Pick 16 (from IND via Sauce Gardner trade)

needs.reason: Biggest need by a wide margin; desperately need playmakers for the offense
Traded Jermaine Johnson to Titans; were 31st in sacks last season; pass rush is nonexistent
Traded away Sauce Gardner; need to rebuild the secondary despite Fitzpatrick addition

ownership.name: Michael Bidwill
ownership.style: Generally hands-off on football decisions but willing to make big coaching changes
ownership.tendencies: Fired Jonathan Gannon after 3-14 season; trusted GM Ossenfort to find new HC despite hot seat speculation

gm.name: Monti Ossenfort
gm.philosophy: Willing to trade down from premium picks if value dictates; aggressive roster overhaul approach
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — modern analytics background from Tennessee Titans front office

head_coach.name: Mike LaFleur
head_coach.scheme_offense: Shanahan-style outside zone scheme, play-action heavy, RPOs
head_coach.scheme_defense: unverified — new defensive coordinator hire

decision_makers.primary: Monti Ossenfort (GM) — on the hot seat, massive draft for his tenure

roster_2026.qb_situation: Widely expected to move on from Kyler Murray; hosting QBs Carson Beck (Miami) and Ty Simpson (Alabama) on pre-draft visits; could draft QB at #3
key_signings: unverified — some O-line and pass rush additions in free agency

needs.reason: Moving on from Kyler Murray; need franchise quarterback for new coaching regime; hosting top QB prospects
Finished 27th in pressure rate and 28th in sacks despite investing resources in pass rush last offseason
Right tackle position unresolved with Jonah Williams and Kelvin Beachum unsigned; two first-round picks spent on OL still hasn't fixed the unit

ownership.name: Amy Adams Strunk
ownership.style: Willing to invest but NFLPA grade dropped from B+ to C in 2026; patient with rebuilds
ownership.tendencies: Interviewed 16 HC candidates before hiring Saleh; trusts GM Borgonzi (rated B+ by players)

gm.name: Mike Borgonzi
gm.philosophy: Needs a home-run draft; willing to let the board come to them given premium cap space and multiple needs
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — learned under Kansas City's analytics-forward approach

head_coach.name: Robert Saleh
head_coach.scheme_offense: OC Brian Daboll; West Coast/Erhardt-Perkins system; designed to develop young QB Cam Ward
head_coach.scheme_defense: 4-3 wide-nine front, Cover 3 base; aggressive use of edge rushers; requires rangy linebacker to roam the middle

decision_makers.primary: Mike Borgonzi (GM) with Robert Saleh (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Cam Ward is the franchise QB from 2025 draft; clear mandate to surround him with weapons
key_signings: Jermaine Johnson II (EDGE, acquired via trade from Jets)

needs.reason: Need elite pass rusher to pair with Jeffery Simmons; Saleh's defense demands dominant edge presence; top EDGE prospects Reese/Bailey/Bain all in play
Clear mandate to add weapons for Cam Ward; receiving corps needs significant upgrade
Transition to Saleh's defense requires more rangy linebacker to roam middle of the field; must be addressed

ownership.name: John Mara and Steve Tisch (co-owners)
ownership.style: Legacy ownership; loyal to staff but willing to make major coaching hires; patience wears thin after sustained losing
ownership.tendencies: Hired John Harbaugh despite other openings available; shows commitment to franchise-changing hires

gm.name: Joe Schoen
gm.tenure: 5th year (hired 2022); power has shifted — must work directly with Harbaugh
gm.philosophy: Aggressive roster construction around Jaxson Dart; willing to trade down from #5 to acquire more picks (only two top-100 selections currently)
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — modern front office sensibilities paired with traditional scouting

head_coach.name: John Harbaugh
head_coach.tenure: 1st year (hired January 20, 2026 — 21st head coach in Giants history)
head_coach.scheme_offense: Versatile system designed around Jaxson Dart; Harbaugh compared Dart to Lamar Jackson on film; strong run-game foundation
head_coach.scheme_defense: Multiple front; aggressive blitz packages; Baltimore-style physical defense

decision_makers.primary: Joe Schoen (GM) and John Harbaugh (HC) — collaborative with Harbaugh holding significant influence

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jaxson Dart is the franchise QB (2025 draft pick); Harbaugh's #1 reason for choosing Giants; second-year development critical
key_signings: Isaiah Likely (TE, from Ravens followed Harbaugh)
expiring_contracts: Malik Nabers (WR — coming off torn ACL in Week 4 of 2025, lost year after great rookie season)

needs.reason: Worst in NFL allowing 5.3 yards per carry; interior defensive line must be addressed for Harbaugh's physical defense
Protecting and developing Jaxson Dart is top priority; Harbaugh wants to build from the trenches
Malik Nabers recovering from torn ACL; need reliable targets while Nabers gets healthy; only two top-100 picks limits options

ownership.name: Jimmy and Dee Haslam
ownership.style: Aggressive, impatient owners; willing to spend big and take risks (e.g., Deshaun Watson contract)
ownership.tendencies: Historically quick to fire coaches and GMs; have cycled through many regimes; analytics-driven under Berry

gm.name: Andrew Berry
gm.philosophy: Heavy analytics approach; values draft capital accumulation; willing to make bold trades; BPA-oriented
gm.analytics_approach: One of the most analytics-driven front offices in the NFL; Berry has Ivy League background

head_coach.name: Todd Monken
head_coach.scheme_offense: Aggressive downfield passing; spread concepts; play-action heavy; known for high-octane offenses (Georgia OC background)
head_coach.scheme_defense: unverified — new DC hire

decision_makers.primary: Andrew Berry (GM/EVP of Football Operations)

roster_2026.qb_situation: unverified — post-Watson era; likely looking at young QB development

picks: Round 1 Pick 24 (from JAX via Travis Hunter trade)

needs.reason: Receiving corps headlined by Jerry Jeudy, Cedric Tillman, and Isaiah Bond is insufficient for Monken's air-attack offense; top need at either #6 or #24
Had to completely overhaul offensive line this offseason; still have a hole at left tackle
Secondary needs reinforcement to complement rebuilt offensive roster

ownership.name: Josh Harris (Harris-Blitzer Sports & Entertainment)
ownership.style: New ownership (acquired 2023); professional sports management background; invested in long-term build
ownership.tendencies: Hired experienced football people; patient with rebuild; significant offseason spending authorized

gm.name: Adam Peters
gm.philosophy: San Francisco model — build through the draft, supplement in free agency; aggressive free agent spending (signed 15 new players this offseason)
gm.analytics_approach: Modern hybrid — SF 49ers analytical background

head_coach.name: Dan Quinn
head_coach.scheme_offense: unverified — new OC system
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Daronte Jones (from Minnesota Vikings); aggressive man-coverage scheme

decision_makers.primary: Adam Peters (GM) — aggressive roster builder

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jayden Daniels is the franchise QB; goal is to surround him with elite weapons and protection
key_departures: Marshon Lattimore (CB, injury concerns, possible cut candidate)
key_signings: 15 total new free agent signings including pass rusher, TE, and RB additions

needs.reason: Need alpha receiver opposite Terry McLaurin; WR room is biggest roster weakness; Carnell Tate (Ohio State) getting heavy attention with Pro Day visit and top-30 visit
Marshon Lattimore no longer the same player after injuries; perimeter CB is a clear need; defense needs to get younger
Released Tyler Biadasz; Nick Allegretti is stopgap; Quinn left door open for draft additions at center

ownership.name: Gayle Benson
ownership.style: Loyal to long-tenured executives; historically big spenders in free agency
ownership.tendencies: Kept Mickey Loomis through decades of cap gymnastics; loyalty to proven leaders

gm.name: Mickey Loomis
gm.philosophy: Aggressive cap management (cap gymnastics); win-now mentality; willing to restructure contracts aggressively
gm.analytics_approach: Traditional scouting with some modern elements; values veteran free agents heavily

head_coach.name: Kellen Moore
head_coach.scheme_offense: Spread concepts, motion-heavy, play-action passing; modern offensive mind
head_coach.scheme_defense: unverified

decision_makers.primary: Mickey Loomis (GM)

roster_2026.qb_situation: Tyler Shough is the starter; organization views him as the franchise guy going into next season
roster_2026.cap_space: Historically tight cap situation; Loomis continues aggressive restructuring

needs.reason: Need a No. 2 receiver to pair with existing weapons; easy pick at #8 if top WR available; Carnell Tate frequently mocked
Pass rush needs significant help; if David Bailey or Rueben Bain Jr. are on the board, they would be hard to pass up
Cornerback is a top-3 need; secondary needs reinforcement to compete in NFC South

ownership.name: Clark Hunt
ownership.style: Hands-off on football decisions; trusts Andy Reid and Brett Veach completely; stability-oriented
ownership.tendencies: Longest-tenured HC/GM combo in NFL; patient ownership that values sustained success; rarely meddles in draft

gm.name: Brett Veach
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader (grew from 5 to 9 picks via trades this offseason); BPA with positional value awareness; willing to make bold moves
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — modern analytics with strong scouting department; known for strategic deception in draft war rooms

head_coach.name: Andy Reid
head_coach.scheme_offense: West Coast offense with creative formations; RPOs; heavy use of TEs and mismatches; Travis Kelce-era passing concepts
head_coach.scheme_defense: 4-3 base; aggressive blitz packages; need to rebuild secondary after major departures

decision_makers.primary: Brett Veach (GM) with Andy Reid (HC) having significant input

roster_2026.qb_situation: Patrick Mahomes — locked in as franchise QB for the foreseeable future
key_departures: Jaylen Watson (CB, lost in free agency)
roster_2026.cap_space: unverified — typical tight cap management under Veach

picks: Round 1 Pick 29 (from LAR via McDuffie trade)

needs.reason: Lost both starting corners — Trent McDuffie (traded) and Jaylen Watson (free agency); enormous hole in secondary
No logical starter opposite George Karlaftis; need complement to Chris Jones interior pressure
Offensive tackle depth is a concern; need to protect Mahomes long-term

ownership.name: Mike Brown
ownership.style: Notoriously frugal; family-run operation; historically reluctant to spend in free agency
ownership.tendencies: Loyal to coaches despite losing — retained Zac Taylor after 6-11 season and three straight years without playoffs; no traditional GM title in organization

gm.name: Duke Tobin (Director of Player Personnel — de facto GM)
gm.philosophy: Draft-and-develop; conservative free agency spending; relies heavily on scouting department
gm.analytics_approach: Traditional scouting emphasis; one of the less analytics-driven front offices

head_coach.name: Zac Taylor
head_coach.scheme_offense: Spread passing attack built around Joe Burrow; three-wide sets; Sean McVay tree concepts
head_coach.scheme_defense: 4-3 base; zone coverage; needs significant talent infusion across all levels

decision_makers.primary: Mike Brown (owner) has final say; Duke Tobin (personnel) leads scouting and draft board

roster_2026.qb_situation: Joe Burrow — locked in as franchise QB; elite talent but team around him has deteriorated
key_departures: Trey Hendrickson (EDGE, signed with Ravens for $112M/4yr)

needs.reason: Pressing need; in prime position to address at #10 with Mansoor Delane (LSU) or Jermod McCoy (Tennessee) likely available; Round 1 priority after signing Mafe at EDGE
Defense needs help at every level; linebacker corps is thin
Defensive line needs reinforcement despite Jonathan Allen signing; overall defensive talent significantly lacking

ownership.name: Stephen Ross
ownership.style: Aggressive owner willing to spend; has been hands-on in major decisions; win-now mentality
ownership.tendencies: Complete organizational overhaul — fired coach and GM; hired new HC and GM; traded away multiple franchise players in rebuild

gm.name: Jon-Eric Sullivan
gm.philosophy: Complete rebuild approach; stripped roster of high-paid veterans; accumulating draft capital and cap space
gm.analytics_approach: unverified — new to role

head_coach.name: Jeff Hafley
head_coach.scheme_offense: unverified — new system being installed
head_coach.scheme_defense: Hafley is a defensive specialist; likely aggressive man-coverage scheme from his background

decision_makers.primary: Jon-Eric Sullivan (GM) with Jeff Hafley (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Malik Willis signed as starter (previously backup in Green Bay); bridge QB at best; long-term answer still needed
key_signings: Darren Waller (TE, came out of retirement)
roster_2026.cap_space: Significant cap space from veteran departures; full rebuild mode

needs.reason: Lost both Tyreek Hill and Jaylen Waddle; Willis needs a reliable WR1; Carnell Tate frequently mocked at #11
Austin Jackson on final year of contract with inconsistent play and injuries; need long-term partner with Patrick Paul
Traded away Jalen Ramsey; cornerback room was already thin before the trade; must rebuild secondary

ownership.name: Jerry Jones
ownership.style: Extremely hands-on; acts as owner AND general manager; most involved owner in NFL history
ownership.tendencies: Meddles in every football decision; loves splash picks and star players; values marketability alongside talent; oldest owner-GM model in NFL

gm.name: Jerry Jones (owner/president/GM) with Will McClay (VP of Player Personnel) as de facto draft evaluator
gm.tenure: Jerry Jones since 1989; Will McClay elevated to key role circa 2014
gm.philosophy: BPA-oriented; Schottenheimer says 'draft natural, draft pure'; aggressive trade history (acquired Micah Parsons from Packers for 1st-round pick + Kenny Clark)
gm.analytics_approach: Traditional with some modern elements; Will McClay is widely respected as one of best talent evaluators in NFL

head_coach.name: Brian Schottenheimer
head_coach.scheme_offense: Pro-style with zone-run elements; play-action passing; physical identity
head_coach.scheme_defense: New-look defense in 2026; multiple fronts with Micah Parsons as centerpiece

decision_makers.primary: Jerry Jones (owner/GM) — always has final say

roster_2026.qb_situation: Dak Prescott — unverified current status but expected franchise QB
key_departures: Kenny Clark (DT, traded to Packers in Micah Parsons deal)
key_signings: Micah Parsons (LB/EDGE, acquired from Packers via trade)

picks: Round 1 Pick 20 (from Green Bay via Parsons trade)

needs.reason: Known top need; seven corners among 30 pre-draft visitors; allowed 8.1 yards per attempt (31st) and ranked near bottom in every pass defense category in 2025
Despite Parsons acquisition, linebacker depth needs reinforcement; South Carolina's Brandon Cisse and Tennessee's Colton Hood in play at #20
Lost Kenny Clark in Parsons trade; defensive interior needs attention; Mansoor Delane (CB) could address CB need instead

ownership.name: Stan Kroenke
ownership.style: Hands-off on football operations; trusts McVay/Snead completely; willing to fund aggressive moves
ownership.tendencies: Supports all-in approach; backed Super Bowl-winning aggressive trading philosophy

gm.name: Les Snead
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader; 'F them picks' mentality but has matured; brought in Phil Savage as personnel expert; flexible approach at #13
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — willing to use analytics but trusts scouting heavily; known for creative roster construction

head_coach.name: Sean McVay
head_coach.scheme_offense: Shanahan-tree outside zone; play-action heavy; motion and misdirection; elite scheme design
head_coach.scheme_defense: Raheem Morris departed; new DC Raheem Morris was replaced (unverified current DC); 3-4 transition

decision_makers.primary: Les Snead (GM) and Sean McVay (HC) — tightly aligned partnership

roster_2026.qb_situation: Matthew Stafford — veteran franchise QB; windows may be closing due to age
key_departures: unverified — traded original 1st, 5th, and 6th round picks to Chiefs for Trent McDuffie

acquired_extra: Round 1 Pick 13 (from Atlanta — via 2025 draft trade for James Pearce Jr.)

needs.reason: Receiver help needed for Stafford; McVay's offense demands elite route runners and playmakers
Offensive tackle depth needed to protect aging Stafford; potential Trent Williams uncertainty applies across NFC West
Tight end need identified by analysts; McVay's system utilizes TEs effectively when talent is available

ownership.name: Steve Bisciotti
ownership.style: Hands-off on football decisions; trusts front office completely; stable ownership
ownership.tendencies: Patient with coaching transitions; allowed Harbaugh to leave for Giants; empowered DeCosta for rebuild

gm.name: Eric DeCosta
gm.philosophy: Draft-centric; values positional versatility; aggressive in trades; BPA-oriented with need awareness
gm.analytics_approach: Heavy analytics — one of the most data-driven front offices in the NFL; pioneered modern draft value charts

head_coach.name: Jesse Minter
head_coach.tenure: 1st year (hired January 2026 — replaced John Harbaugh after 18 years)
head_coach.scheme_offense: unverified — new OC hire
head_coach.scheme_defense: Minter is a defensive specialist; aggressive scheme; built Michigan's #1 defense in 2023; multiple fronts with exotic blitz packages

decision_makers.primary: Eric DeCosta (GM) — now fully driving team-building without Harbaugh

roster_2026.qb_situation: Lamar Jackson — franchise QB, MVP-caliber; need to surround him with better weapons
key_departures: Tyler Linderbaum (C, signed with Raiders for record $81M/3yr)
Maxx Crosby trade fell through (medical concerns)

acquired_extra: Multiple late-round picks from Crosby trade reversal

needs.reason: WRs totaled only 1,946 receiving yards (25th in NFL); Lamar Jackson desperately needs better pass catchers; Jordyn Tyson linked
Lost Tyler Linderbaum (3x Pro Bowl C) in free agency; center spot vacant; need guard competition for Emery Jones at RG
Lost both Isaiah Likely and Charlie Kolar in free agency; depth chart depleted to Durham Smythe and one other contributor

ownership.name: Glazer Family (Joel, Bryan, and Edward Glazer)
ownership.style: Generally hands-off; trust front office; stable ownership group
ownership.tendencies: Patient with coaching staff; kept Todd Bowles despite missing playoffs; invest in roster when competitive window is open

gm.name: Jason Licht
gm.philosophy: Values trenches and defense; willing to make aggressive trades; drafted well in recent years
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — traditional scouting roots with increasing analytics integration

head_coach.name: Todd Bowles
head_coach.scheme_offense: OC Zac Robinson (from Atlanta); 12 personnel heavy (two tight ends, 38.2% usage in ATL — 2nd most in NFL)
head_coach.scheme_defense: 3-4 base; aggressive blitz packages; Bowles is a defensive mastermind; known for exotic pressure looks

decision_makers.primary: Jason Licht (GM) with Todd Bowles (HC) input

roster_2026.qb_situation: Baker Mayfield — unverified current status but expected starter
key_departures: Jamel Dean (CB, left in free agency)

needs.reason: GM Licht explicitly stated at Combine they 'need to get more pressure on the quarterback'; Akheem Mesidor, Keldric Faulk, Cashius Howell all options at #15
Lavonte David retired — franchise legend gone; need replacement at inside linebacker
Lost Jamel Dean in free agency; secondary needs reinforcement to complement Bowles' aggressive scheme

ownership.name: Sheila Ford Hamp
ownership.style: Supportive of front office; trusts Campbell and Holmes; patient with process
ownership.tendencies: Backed the rebuild fully; loyalty to coaching staff through ups and downs; will apply pressure after 2025 disappointment

gm.name: Brad Holmes
gm.philosophy: BPA-focused; values offensive line and trenches; excellent mid-round draft evaluator; built a contender quickly
gm.analytics_approach: Heavy analytics — Rams analytical tree; data-driven draft board construction

head_coach.name: Dan Campbell
head_coach.scheme_offense: OC Drew Petzing; power run game with play-action; physical identity; creative formations
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Kelvin Sheppard; 4-3 base; aggressive front seven; Aidan Hutchinson as centerpiece

decision_makers.primary: Brad Holmes (GM) and Dan Campbell (HC) — tightly aligned

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jared Goff — franchise QB; under contract long-term
key_departures: unverified — division finish disappointment after consecutive deep playoff runs

needs.reason: Sizable void at left tackle; 62 of 66 mock drafts pair Lions with offensive lineman in Round 1; need pro-ready tackle to reignite dominant rushing attack
Need young pass rusher opposite Aidan Hutchinson; DJ Wonnum is a veteran stopgap, not long-term answer
Secondary needs reinforcement; Campbell wants competition at multiple positions

ownership.name: Zygi Wilf and Mark Wilf
ownership.style: Generally patient but willing to make dramatic moves; invested in winning
ownership.tendencies: Fired Kwesi Adofo-Mensah just 243 days after extending him (spent $269.5M); disconnect between personnel and coaching was dealbreaker

gm.name: Rob Brzezinski (Interim — EVP serving as acting GM)
gm.tenure: Interim since January 2026; longtime executive; permanent GM search postponed until after draft
gm.philosophy: Maintaining status quo; not expected to make dramatic moves; coaching staff driving draft preferences
gm.analytics_approach: Organization was analytics-heavy under Adofo-Mensah; direction may shift with new GM

head_coach.name: Kevin O'Connell
head_coach.scheme_offense: OC Wes Phillips; McVay-tree scheme; play-action, outside zone, motion-heavy; built around passing game
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Brian Flores; aggressive man-coverage; heavy blitz packages; Flores is one of most aggressive DCs in NFL

decision_makers.primary: Kevin O'Connell (HC) and Brian Flores (DC) likely have outsized influence with no permanent GM

roster_2026.qb_situation: Kyler Murray acquired (unverified — linked in search results) or J.J. McCarthy situation; QB situation is unsettled
key_signings: Frank Smith (assistant HC, former Dolphins OC)
expiring_contracts: TJ Hockenson (TE, restructured but gone after 2026)

needs.reason: Harrison Smith could retire at any time; Dillon Thieneman (Oregon) has become consensus mock pick — would be Smith's heir; mock-draft darling for Vikings
Defensive tackle is a major need; interior defensive line requires reinforcement for Flores' aggressive scheme
Cornerback depth needs improvement; secondary talent must match Flores' aggressive man-coverage demands

ownership.name: David Tepper
ownership.style: Aggressive, impatient owner; willing to spend but historically poor coaching hires
ownership.tendencies: Has fired multiple coaches quickly; finally seeing stability with Canales; won NFC South at 8-9 — first division title since 2015

gm.name: Dan Morgan
gm.philosophy: Seeks best value; willing to move up and down draft board; tries to land prospects he likes regardless of positional need
gm.analytics_approach: unverified — relatively new GM; building his evaluation framework

head_coach.name: Dave Canales
head_coach.scheme_offense: West Coast concepts; developing Bryce Young (unverified QB situation); QB-friendly system
head_coach.scheme_defense: Multiple fronts; need more talent across all levels of defense

decision_makers.primary: Dan Morgan (GM) with Dave Canales (HC) and EVP Brandt Tillis collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: unverified — Young development or new addition; upward trajectory under Canales

needs.reason: Need edge rusher so badly; positional value makes it far and away the biggest need; almost assuredly taking best EDGE at 19 despite Phillips acquisition
Canales said 'a couple of dynamic safeties could free us up' — sees opportunity for impact player at #19
Defense needs to get younger and more talented across the secondary; playoff team needs upgrades to compete

ownership.name: Art Rooney II
ownership.style: Old-school stability; Rooney family has owned Steelers since 1933; patient with coaching hires
ownership.tendencies: Values loyalty and culture; typically promotes from within or hires proven coaches; rarely makes impulsive moves

gm.name: Omar Khan
gm.philosophy: Draft-and-develop; strong salary cap management; values picks and competitive contracts
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — increasing analytics adoption under Khan compared to Kevin Colbert era

head_coach.name: Mike McCarthy
head_coach.scheme_offense: Pro-style West Coast offense; play-action heavy; values run-pass balance; Super Bowl-winning HC (Green Bay)
head_coach.scheme_defense: Steelers traditional 3-4; aggressive blitz packages; zone coverage concepts

decision_makers.primary: Omar Khan (GM) with Mike McCarthy (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: unverified — likely Russell Wilson or new acquisition situation
key_signings: Michael Pittman Jr. (WR, acquired via trade from Colts)

needs.reason: Despite Pittman and Metcalf trades, WR3 Roman Wilson has only 164 career yards; McCarthy needs depth for his offense
Second most pressing need; three safeties with first-round grades in this class; prime position at #21 for day-one starter
Draft is deep at multiple positions Steelers need including offensive line; must protect QB investment

ownership.name: Dean Spanos
ownership.style: Generally hands-off on football decisions; trusts front office
ownership.tendencies: Patient with coaching staff; backed Harbaugh's aggressive roster construction

gm.name: Joe Hortiz
gm.philosophy: BPA regardless of roster needs — drafted Joe Alt despite WR need (2024), RB Omarion Hampton despite OL need (2025); true board discipline
gm.analytics_approach: Baltimore Ravens scouting tree — heavy analytics integration

head_coach.name: Jim Harbaugh
head_coach.scheme_offense: Power run game; play-action; physical identity; pro-style formations
head_coach.scheme_defense: Multiple fronts; Jesse Minter departed for Ravens HC job; new DC unverified

decision_makers.primary: Joe Hortiz (GM) — follows his board regardless of need

roster_2026.qb_situation: Justin Herbert — elite franchise QB; locked in long-term
key_departures: Zion Johnson (G, left in free agency)

needs.reason: No clear starter at left guard after Zion Johnson departure; Strange at RG and Biadasz at C are new, but LG is a hole
Lack depth beyond Khalil Mack and Tuipulotu; need young pass rusher for future
Interior defensive line needs reinforcement; missing space-eating presence in middle

ownership.name: Jeffrey Lurie
ownership.style: Supportive of front office; backs Roseman's aggressive approach; willing to spend
ownership.tendencies: Patient with coaching changes; trusts Howie Roseman's unconventional methods; values Super Bowl windows

gm.name: Howie Roseman (EVP of Football Operations)
gm.tenure: 15th+ year — one of the longest-tenured GMs in the NFL
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader and cap manipulator; builds from the inside out (trenches first); edge rusher is his most-drafted position in Round 1
gm.analytics_approach: Heavy analytics — one of the most aggressive data-driven front offices; innovative contract structures

head_coach.name: Nick Sirianni
head_coach.scheme_offense: RPO-heavy; spread concepts; values skill position versatility
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Vic Fangio; 3-4 base; zone coverage; complex disguised pressures

decision_makers.primary: Howie Roseman — unquestioned decision-maker on roster construction

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jalen Hurts — franchise QB; need to protect him and improve weapons
roster_2026.cap_space: unverified — Roseman typically manages cap aggressively

needs.reason: Uncharacteristically poor OL in 2025; Lane Johnson and Landon Dickerson both pondered retirement; majority of pre-draft visits are with OL prospects
Lost Josh Sweat and Jaelan Phillips over last two offseasons; Nolan Smith and Jalyx Hunt have flashed but group lacks elite bite; Roseman's most-drafted position in Round 1
Lost Reed Blankenship in free agency; only Drew Mukuba returning for second season; safety position feels unfinished

ownership.name: George McCaskey (controlling owner, took over from Virginia Halas McCaskey who passed February 2025)
ownership.style: Family ownership since franchise founding; historically conservative; improving under new generation
ownership.tendencies: Hired Ben Johnson as HC — first aggressive coaching hire in years; invested in franchise QB

gm.name: Ryan Poles
gm.philosophy: Build through the draft; aggressive early-round picks; values athletic profiles and upside
gm.analytics_approach: Modern analytics-forward front office; Poles came from Chiefs analytical background

head_coach.name: Ben Johnson
head_coach.scheme_offense: Creative play-calling; Lions-style power run with play-action; motion-heavy; designed to develop QB
head_coach.scheme_defense: unverified DC; defense was 29th in total defense (361.8 YPG allowed) in 2025

decision_makers.primary: Ryan Poles (GM) with Ben Johnson (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Caleb Williams (2024 #1 pick) — franchise QB; Johnson hired specifically to develop him
key_signings: Coby Bryant (S, from Seahawks Super Bowl champ)

acquired_extra: Extra 2nd-round pick (from Colts in DJ Moore trade)

needs.reason: Every mock draft has Bears targeting pass rusher at #25; 29th in total defense in 2025; desperately need pressure
Ozzy Trapilo's injury and uncertain timeline could shift Round 1 priority; LT competition features four players with no consistent high-end play recently
Lost both Kevin Byard and Jaquan Brisker; Coby Bryant signing fills one spot but second safety vacancy remains

ownership.name: Terry and Kim Pegula
ownership.style: Invested owners; willing to spend; demanded 'next level' after repeated playoff disappointments
ownership.tendencies: Fired McDermott after 4th divisional round exit in 5 years despite winning seasons; want Super Bowl, not just playoffs

gm.name: Brandon Beane
gm.philosophy: Smart free agent spending for flexibility; values draft capital; made veteran signings this offseason to create draft flexibility
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — strong analytical framework with traditional scouting values

head_coach.name: Joe Brady
head_coach.scheme_offense: Modern spread concepts; creative play-design for Josh Allen; RPOs and read-options; was Bills QBs coach (2022), interim OC (2023), then OC (2024-25)
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Jim Leonhard; aggressive scheme requiring downhill linebackers who can blitz

decision_makers.primary: Brandon Beane (GM) with Joe Brady (HC) collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Josh Allen — elite franchise QB; perennial MVP candidate; organization built around him
key_departures: Bradley Chubb (EDGE, signed from Dolphins via FA)
key_signings: Other veteran free agents for roster flexibility

needs.reason: Terrel Bernard and Dorian Williams may not fit Jim Leonhard's scheme; needs more aggressive, downhill players who can blitz
Consistent pass rush has been an issue nearly every year of Josh Allen era; Leonhard's scheme demands it; Chubb helps but more needed
Missing true space-eating nose tackle for Leonhard's defense; have surplus of DEs who can play DT but no pure nose

ownership.name: Jed York
ownership.style: Trusts Kyle Shanahan and John Lynch completely; backs aggressive roster moves; win-now window
ownership.tendencies: Stable ownership that supports long-term vision; willing to trade picks for proven players

gm.name: John Lynch
gm.philosophy: Aggressive roster construction; values scheme fits over raw talent; heavy free agent spending when in win-now mode
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — strong analytics with Shanahan's schematic evaluation driving choices

head_coach.name: Kyle Shanahan
head_coach.scheme_offense: Wide zone run scheme; play-action heavy; motion and misdirection; among the best offensive minds in NFL
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Raheem Morris hired (replacing Robert Saleh who left); transitioning to 3-4 front

decision_makers.primary: John Lynch (GM) and Kyle Shanahan (HC) — co-equal partnership

roster_2026.qb_situation: Brock Purdy — franchise QB; under contract; system quarterback thriving in Shanahan's scheme
key_departures: Jauan Jennings (WR, set to be FA)
roster_2026.cap_space: unverified — aggressive spending in win-now mode

needs.reason: Trent Williams uncertainty at LT; guard is a weakness (Burford struggling, Puni regressed, Bartch injured); interior OL needs help
Only 20 sacks in 2025 — six fewer than the lowly Jets; pass rush was anemic; edge rusher in play at #27
Surprisingly big need with switch to 3-4 defense under Raheem Morris; need different body types for new scheme

ownership.name: Cal McNair (chairman/CEO)
ownership.style: Generally supportive of football operations; invested in winning; family ownership (Janice McNair holds title of owner)
ownership.tendencies: Backed DeMeco Ryans and Nick Caserio; patient with rebuild that turned to contention quickly

gm.name: Nick Caserio
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader (7 trades in recent draft); values flexibility and draft capital accumulation; Belichick tree
gm.analytics_approach: New England analytical roots; data-driven with traditional scouting

head_coach.name: DeMeco Ryans
head_coach.scheme_offense: Spread concepts; building around C.J. Stroud; multiple TE sets
head_coach.scheme_defense: Aggressive 4-3; man-coverage heavy; exotic pressures; former 49ers DC — physical defensive identity

decision_makers.primary: Nick Caserio (EVP/GM) with DeMeco Ryans (HC) input

roster_2026.qb_situation: C.J. Stroud — elite young franchise QB; built to win now around him
key_departures: unverified — 12-win season indicates roster mostly intact
expiring_contracts: Ed Ingram (RG, due for new contract)

needs.reason: Most glaring need is interior offensive line; need upgrade over Jake Andrews at center or find new guard; Olaivavega Ioane (Penn State) frequently mocked
Interior defensive line needs reinforcement alongside offense; DeMeco Ryans wants dominant interior presence
Linebacker depth needs attention; Ryans' defense requires versatile, athletic linebackers

ownership.name: Robert Kraft
ownership.style: Hands-on owner who demands excellence; hired Vrabel — a former Patriots player — signaling return to Patriot Way
ownership.tendencies: Loyal but demands results; backed Belichick for 24 years; now expects quicker turnaround under Vrabel

gm.name: Eliot Wolf (EVP of Player Personnel)
gm.philosophy: Draft-and-develop; building around Drake Maye; values offensive line and defense
gm.analytics_approach: Modern approach — Wolf comes from Green Bay Packers and Cleveland Browns front office trees

head_coach.name: Mike Vrabel
head_coach.scheme_offense: Physical identity; values tight ends (explicitly said at league meetings he hopes to draft a TE); run-first approach
head_coach.scheme_defense: Multiple fronts; aggressive blitz packages; Vrabel is a defensive-minded coach

decision_makers.primary: Eliot Wolf (EVP) with Mike Vrabel (HC) having significant input

roster_2026.qb_situation: Drake Maye — 2nd year franchise QB; development is top priority; need to protect him
expiring_contracts: Isaiah Wynn (OT, Moses — starter whose successor is needed)

needs.reason: Draft is extremely shallow at tackle; need Moses's replacement in-house; can't envision doing it without using #31 on a tackle successor; LT Will Campbell's depth behind him is thin
Need pass-rush help badly; draft is deeper at EDGE than tackle; could address at #31 or early Day 2
Vrabel explicitly said at NFL annual meeting he hopes to add a tight end in the draft; position is a priority for his physical offense

ownership.name: Jody Allen (Paul G. Allen Trust)
ownership.style: Continuation of Paul Allen's vision; trusts football operations; supportive of aggressive moves
ownership.tendencies: Backed the Super Bowl-winning roster construction; willing to trade picks for proven talent

gm.name: John Schneider
gm.tenure: 17th year (since 2010) — one of the longest-tenured GMs
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader; values athletic traits and competition; built Super Bowl dynasty through mid-round gems; willing to trade up or down
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — strong analytics with Pete Carroll's influence giving way to modern approach under Macdonald

head_coach.name: Mike Macdonald
head_coach.scheme_offense: unverified OC — new coordinator needed
head_coach.scheme_defense: Macdonald is a defensive specialist; multiple fronts; exotic blitz packages; Baltimore-tree scheme

ownership.name: Jim Irsay
ownership.style: Passionate, hands-on owner; publicly vocal about team direction; willing to make bold roster moves
ownership.tendencies: Patient with Ballard despite declining results; values stability but demands playoffs; has been known to meddle

gm.name: Chris Ballard
gm.philosophy: Build through the draft; historically conservative in free agency; values defensive players and offensive linemen; BPA approach
gm.analytics_approach: Traditional scouting with some analytics; less aggressive than peer GMs

head_coach.name: Shane Steichen
head_coach.scheme_offense: Spread concepts; RPOs; developed Jalen Hurts in Philly; building around Anthony Richardson (or successor)

ownership.name: Arthur Blank
ownership.style: Engaged owner who cares about culture and fan experience; willing to spend on coaching and roster
ownership.tendencies: Fired Raheem Morris after missing playoffs since 2017; brought in Stefanski and Matt Ryan (president of football) for fresh start

gm.name: Ian Cunningham
gm.tenure: 1st year (hired January 29, 2026 — formerly Bears assistant GM)
gm.philosophy: Rebuilding with limited draft capital (5 picks); must maximize value on every selection
gm.analytics_approach: Modern front office background from Bears and Eagles organizations

head_coach.scheme_offense: Stefanski runs a balanced offense — outside zone, play-action, RPOs; values tight ends and run game; Tua vs Penix QB battle brewing

Mooney's exorbitant cost makes him a likely cut; need receivers for Stefanski's offense regardless of QB

ownership.name: Green Bay Packers Inc. (publicly owned — only community-owned NFL franchise)
ownership.style: Board of Directors oversees; Mark Murphy is president/CEO; unique community ownership model
ownership.tendencies: Stability-oriented; trust front office and coaching staff; organic development approach

gm.name: Brian Gutekunst
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader — acquired Micah Parsons from Dallas, giving up 1st-round pick + Kenny Clark; values impact players; willing to sacrifice draft capital for proven talent
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — Ted Thompson's traditional scouting legacy blended with modern analytics

head_coach.scheme_offense: Shanahan-tree outside zone; play-action heavy; motion and pre-snap shifts; RPOs

ownership.name: Shahid Khan
ownership.style: Patient owner; willing to invest in coaching and roster; backed rebuild and now expecting sustained success
ownership.tendencies: Hired entirely new coaching and GM staff in 2025; supported aggressive 2025 draft (Travis Hunter trade-up); expects playoffs

gm.name: James Gladstone
gm.philosophy: BPA approach — roster is good enough to not draft by need; values flexibility; willing to trade up or down; 'stay agile'

ownership.name: Rob Walton (Walton-Penner Family Ownership Group)
ownership.style: Richest NFL owner; invested in winning; professional sports management group; bought team for $4.65B in 2022
ownership.tendencies: Supportive of Payton and Paton; patient with roster construction; backed AFC Championship run

gm.name: George Paton
gm.philosophy: Patient roster builder; retains own players (90%+ of 2025 starting snaps returning); smart trade execution (Waddle acquisition)

head_coach.scheme_offense: Saints-style offense — short passing game, diverse formations, creative play-calling; developing Bo Nix
